meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Ship Faster & Impress Clients (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Stop wasting weeks on boilerplate. Learn to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance development workflow today.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. Using a SaaS starter kit provides pre-configured authentication, payments, and database schemas, slashing development time by 70%. By leveraging AI-driven tools like Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you can ship production-ready SaaS applications in days, not months, while maximizing your freelance profit margins.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re still wrestling with user authentication boilerplate for the tenth time this year. In the high-stakes world of freelance development, every hour spent on repetitive setup is an hour of lost revenue. If you aren't using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you are effectively paying a 'manual labor tax' on every project you take.

But the tide is turning. We are moving beyond manual coding into the era of AI-orchestrated engineering. By combining the structural power of Antigravity with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you can automate the mundane and focus exclusively on the logic that makes your client's product valuable. It’s time to stop coding and start shipping.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust SaaS starter kit template. This provides the scaffolding for your database, auth, and billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements for a B2B SaaS dashboard. Suggest a modular starter kit architecture that supports Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration.

steps.title: Define Schema & Business Logic
steps.description: Map your database entities and core business rules to ensure the starter kit handles your specific data relationships.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application that supports role-based access control (RBAC) and subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update schema.prisma with the generated multi-tenant structure'

steps.title: Configure Auth & Identity
steps.description: Integrate your authentication provider to handle secure logins, magic links, and session management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a secure implementation guide for NextAuth.js within my existing starter kit to enable Google and Email-based authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure NextAuth with the provided providers and secure the API routes'

steps.title: Implement Billing Logic
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your starter kit to handle subscriptions, webhooks, and invoice generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler that syncs subscription status updates back to my Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create webhook handler for subscription.updated and subscription.deleted events'

steps.title: Automated Testing & Deployment
steps.description: Use AI to write unit tests for your critical business logic before deploying to your hosting provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Jest unit tests for the core subscription billing logic to ensure edge cases are covered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and execute unit tests for the billing service'

pro_tips: Always treat your starter kit as a read-only template; keep your modifications in custom modules to simplify future framework updates.
Use Claude Code to audit your dependencies every sprint to ensure no security vulnerabilities have entered the codebase.
Pre-build a set of 'Custom UI Components' that match your design language to ensure your SaaS apps look bespoke, not generic.
Leverage environment variable management tools early to avoid 'secret sprawl' when handing off projects to clients.
Implement a standard CI/CD pipeline immediately; even if the client doesn't ask for it, it makes your service look 10x more professional.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, focus on building your 'Client Portfolio' by shipping three variations of the starter kit to show potential customers your versatility.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to stop being a code-monkey and start being a high-value SaaS architect. Stop struggling with boilerplate and start delivering value that clients actually pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
